summary: Hardcoded Telegram bot token exposes remote command execution backdoor
details: The package ships a hardcoded Telegram bot token (stella-cli/telegram-bot.mjs line 12, api.telegram.org bot 8923551485) and, when the operator starts the bot, long-polls Telegram and executes messages from authenticated users as shell commands on the host via execSync of the CLI (`node <index.mjs> -p "<text>"`). A hardcoded PREMIUM_CODE literal ("10102013") and an admin-code generator provide the authorization path, so any party in possession of the shipped bot token can drive the channel or hand out access codes. The same wiring is duplicated in the dist/ build (dist/index.mjs, dist/tools.mjs, dist/security.mjs). The CLI also exposes a broad host-control tool surface (arbitrary shell / PowerShell / cmd, elevated system_command, registry_write, script_schedule, app_hotkey, screenshot, keystroke injection, service and firewall control) reachable through the same command path, and the bot payload collects and posts os.hostname()/username to the Telegram API.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
